description: Moonshot parameter-golf runner on the executable strong 8192-tokenizer local base. Active packs are process-split, TTT, and deploy-alignment experiments; larger frontier-rebase ideas remain explicitly deferred.

slots.name: ttt_family_control_base
slots.why: Primary control for the rebased pre-quant TTT family pack.
slots.validates: TTT variants are measured against the same strong static trunk.
slots.falsifies: The TTT family pack lacks a stable reference.

slots.name: ttt_family_control_repeat
slots.why: Noise floor for the TTT family pack.
slots.validates: TTT deltas are large enough to rank confidently.
slots.falsifies: Short-horizon variation is too large for this lane.

slots.name: ttt_last2_raw_state
slots.why: Simplest TTT baseline: adapt only the last 2 blocks from the raw final state.
slots.validates: Even a narrow raw-state pre-quant TTT finisher is a live mechanism here.
slots.falsifies: The TTT lane needs stronger state selection or broader adaptation to matter.

slots.name: ttt_last2_ema_state
slots.why: Tests whether the best simple TTT base object is EMA rather than the raw final checkpoint.
slots.validates: EMA remains the right precursor state for narrow pre-quant TTT.
slots.falsifies: TTT largely erases the raw-vs-EMA distinction for this simple finisher.

slots.name: ttt_last2_ema_longer
slots.why: Tests whether the TTT gain is limited more by horizon than by finisher form.
slots.validates: The late TTT lane is compute-starved and benefits from more adaptation even without changing the finisher law.
slots.falsifies: Extra TTT epochs mostly overfit or waste time.

slots.name: ttt_broad_dttt_tail
slots.why: Broader upper-trunk adaptation with blockwise LR decay is the cleanest discriminative-TTT proxy we can support now.
slots.validates: The TTT law matters, not just whether TTT is on.
slots.falsifies: The broader tail adds cost without beating the simpler last-2 finisher.

slots.name: ttt_checkpoint_selected
slots.why: Tests whether the best TTT start state should come from a late deployed-state bakeoff rather than plain EMA.
slots.validates: Checkpoint selection and TTT stack cleanly in the same lane.
slots.falsifies: The choice of starting state is mostly irrelevant once TTT runs.

slots.name: ttt_checkpoint_selected_plus_qat
slots.why: This is the strongest honest bridge from our existing deploy-alignment patches into the new pre-quant TTT lane.
slots.validates: Late deploy shaping still matters when the stage ends with TTT rather than plain export.
slots.falsifies: TTT absorbs most of the benefit and the extra late-QAT machinery is decorative.

slots.name: phase_split_control_base
slots.why: Control for the lead phase-split hailmary pack.
slots.validates: Process-split candidates are measured against the current frontier-aligned default trunk.
slots.falsifies: The lead hailmary pack lacks a stable reference.

slots.name: phase_split_control_repeat
slots.why: Noise floor for the lead phase-split pack.
slots.validates: Small phase-split deltas are interpretable.
slots.falsifies: This pack is too noisy for confident ranking.

slots.name: phase_split_ema_export
slots.why: Simplest runnable test that the late exported state should differ from the last raw checkpoint.
slots.validates: Late-state selection is first-order enough to headline a rebuild pack.
slots.falsifies: Checkpoint-state selection is too small to matter.

slots.name: phase_split_late_qat
slots.why: Internalizes export pressure only late instead of distorting the whole trajectory.
slots.validates: Late deploy alignment is a real lead mechanism.
slots.falsifies: Late export pressure is still too disruptive or too weak.

slots.name: phase_split_late_weight_perturbation
slots.why: Tests a late-only compressibility-shaping regime instead of full-run geometry noise.
slots.validates: The right geometry intervention is phase-specific, not homogeneous across the run.
slots.falsifies: The geometry signal only pays if active throughout training.

slots.name: phase_split_ema_plus_late_qat
slots.why: Tests whether late deploy alignment and exported-state selection stack cleanly.
slots.validates: The rebuild should think in process plus state, not one or the other.
slots.falsifies: One of the two mechanisms mostly shadows the other.

slots.name: phase_split_late_perturb_plus_ema
slots.why: Tests whether a late flatter basin and exported-state averaging reinforce each other.
slots.validates: Late geometry and late state selection attack different parts of the deployed gap.
slots.falsifies: EMA washes out the late geometry signal.

slots.name: phase_split_late_qat_plus_late_perturb
slots.why: Tests a stronger late-only alignment regime that changes both exported robustness and basin geometry.
slots.validates: The strongest late-phase mechanisms are composable enough to become a real moonshot parent.
slots.falsifies: Late-phase mechanisms interact destructively instead of stacking.

slots.name: checkpoint_selection_control_base
slots.why: Control for deployed-checkpoint selection hypotheses.
slots.validates: Checkpoint-selection mechanisms are measured against the same base trunk.
slots.falsifies: This lane lacks a stable anchor.

slots.name: checkpoint_selection_control_repeat
slots.why: Noise floor for checkpoint-selection work.
slots.validates: Small checkpoint-state deltas are interpretable.
slots.falsifies: The lane is too noisy to rank subtle state-selection ideas.

slots.name: checkpoint_selection_ema_export
slots.why: Current runnable proxy for the broader checkpoint-selection family.
slots.validates: There is enough signal here to justify larger state-selection patches.
slots.falsifies: Checkpoint selection is too weak to deserve a lead rebuild lane.
notes: Proxy only. The real family is best-of-last-K deployed-state selection.

slots.name: checkpoint_selection_best_of_last_k_raw
slots.why: Tests whether the best deployed raw snapshot appears before the last step.
slots.validates: Last checkpoint is a false invariant.
slots.falsifies: The final step is already the right export target.

slots.name: checkpoint_selection_best_of_last_k_ema_raw
slots.why: Tests raw-vs-EMA late-snapshot competition instead of assuming EMA is always right.
slots.validates: Export state selection must compare multiple tracked states, not just one.
slots.falsifies: EMA already saturates the useful state-selection story.

slots.name: checkpoint_selection_best_deployed_score
slots.why: Selects the checkpoint by deployed GPTQ score instead of raw validation.
slots.validates: The true export target differs from the raw training optimum.
slots.falsifies: Deployed score peaks at the same point as the raw checkpoint.

slots.name: checkpoint_selection_best_deployed_score_plus_ema
slots.why: Tests whether deployed GPTQ selection still matters after EMA is already tracked.
slots.validates: State selection is more than just 'turn EMA on'.
slots.falsifies: EMA already captures the best deployable state.

slots.name: checkpoint_selection_dual_track_export
slots.why: Tracks raw, EMA, and late-aligned variants in parallel and exports the best deployed artifact.
slots.validates: Export object selection is a real mechanism family, not a minor afterthought.
slots.falsifies: Parallel state tracking is not worth the added complexity.

slots.name: staged_curriculum_control_base
slots.why: Control for phase-split data-order hypotheses.
slots.validates: Curriculum-stage ideas have a stable reference.
slots.falsifies: This lane lacks an interpretable anchor.

slots.name: staged_curriculum_control_repeat
slots.why: Noise floor for staged curriculum.
slots.validates: Small data-order deltas are interpretable.
slots.falsifies: This lane is too noisy to rank.

slots.name: staged_curriculum_reverse_proxy
slots.why: Runnable proxy for the broader staged-curriculum family.
slots.validates: Data order is still first-order enough to deserve a lead rebuild lane.
slots.falsifies: Even a rough order change is too weak to matter.
notes: Proxy only. The real family is phase-dependent order changes.

slots.name: staged_curriculum_size_desc_proxy
slots.why: Second runnable proxy for the staged-curriculum family.
slots.validates: Simple curriculum heuristics are worth replacing with true phase splits.
slots.falsifies: The curriculum lane is too weak even before staging.
notes: Proxy only. The real family is phase-dependent order changes.

slots.name: staged_curriculum_reverse_then_size_desc
slots.why: Starts with broad coverage then shifts to denser/harder shards late.
slots.validates: One shard order for the whole run is a false invariant.
slots.falsifies: Static order is already good enough.

slots.name: staged_curriculum_easy_then_hard
slots.why: Allocates early updates to easy shards and late updates to hard shards.
slots.validates: Difficulty staging matters more than one fixed global order.
slots.falsifies: The train cap is too short for a two-phase curriculum to pay off.

slots.name: staged_curriculum_reverse_then_shuffle
slots.why: Uses ordering only where it buys fast learning, then relaxes to avoid overfitting the order.
slots.validates: Curriculum should be stage-limited rather than permanent.
slots.falsifies: Once order helps, it should stay active throughout training.

slots.name: staged_curriculum_plus_late_qat
slots.why: Tests whether a better late data regime and late deploy alignment reinforce each other.
slots.validates: Process split should couple data order and late objective, not treat them independently.
slots.falsifies: Curriculum and late deploy pressure do not compose.

slots.name: parameter_family_control_base
slots.why: Control for tensor-family split hypotheses.
slots.validates: Family-specific late rules are measured against the base trunk.
slots.falsifies: This lane lacks a stable anchor.

slots.name: parameter_family_control_repeat
slots.why: Noise floor for tensor-family split work.
slots.validates: Small family-split deltas are interpretable.
slots.falsifies: This lane is too noisy to rank.

slots.name: parameter_family_embeddings_head_late_freeze
slots.why: Stops the most fragile parameter families from drifting late while matrices keep consolidating.
slots.validates: Late adaptation should be specialized by tensor family.
slots.falsifies: Homogeneous late adaptation is already fine.

slots.name: parameter_family_matrix_only_late_qat
slots.why: Applies late deploy pressure only to the matrix trunk where quantization damage concentrates.
slots.validates: Family-specific deploy alignment beats homogeneous late QAT.
slots.falsifies: Late deploy pressure needs to hit all trainable weights equally.

slots.name: parameter_family_decay_split
slots.why: Gives embeddings/head/scalars different late regularization than the trunk.
slots.validates: One decay law for all families is a false invariant.
slots.falsifies: The current uniform late law is already near-optimal.

slots.name: parameter_family_lr_warmdown_split
slots.why: Lets different families decay into warmdown at different rates.
slots.validates: Warmdown should be specialized by tensor family.
slots.falsifies: A single warmdown law is already sufficient.

slots.name: parameter_family_value_live_trunk_freeze
slots.why: Keeps value-side or selected late-sensitive paths adapting after the rest of the trunk stabilizes.
slots.validates: Different matrix families should not share one late adaptation rule.
slots.falsifies: Selective late plasticity is unnecessary.

slots.name: parameter_family_freeze_plus_ema
slots.why: Tests whether family-specific late stabilization and exported-state selection stack.
slots.validates: Family specialization and state selection are complementary mechanisms.
slots.falsifies: The freeze gain does not survive export-state averaging.

slots.name: context_stage_control_base
slots.why: Control for context-budget split hypotheses.
slots.validates: Context-stage work is measured against the same base trunk.
slots.falsifies: This lane lacks a stable anchor.

slots.name: context_stage_control_repeat
slots.why: Noise floor for context-stage work.
slots.validates: Small context-stage deltas are interpretable.
slots.falsifies: This lane is too noisy to rank.

slots.name: context_stage_xsa_all_proxy
slots.why: Runnable proxy for the broader context-stage family.
slots.validates: Context treatment is still first-order enough to deserve a lead rebuild lane.
slots.falsifies: Context changes are too weak even before staging.
notes: Proxy only. The real family changes context policy by phase.

slots.name: context_stage_xsa_all_plus_ema_proxy
slots.why: Runnable bridge between context and state-selection lanes.
slots.validates: Context changes are worth carrying into the rebuilt context-stage family.
slots.falsifies: The current context signal is too brittle to survive export-state smoothing.
notes: Proxy only. The real family changes context policy by phase.

slots.name: context_stage_short_then_long
slots.why: Allocates early budget to cheaper shorter-context learning, then turns on the heavy context regime late.
slots.validates: One context budget for the whole run is a false invariant.
slots.falsifies: The long-context regime should stay on the whole time.

slots.name: context_stage_xsa_last4_then_all
slots.why: Starts with a cheaper partial context specialization and only pays for XSA-all late.
slots.validates: Context specialization should itself be phased.
slots.falsifies: The best context stack should just be on from step zero.

slots.name: context_stage_short_then_long_plus_late_qat
slots.why: Pairs a phased context budget with late deploy alignment.
slots.validates: Context staging and late deploy pressure are complementary rather than competing for budget.
slots.falsifies: The two process splits do not compose.

slots.name: context_stage_xsa_then_full_gptq
slots.why: Tests whether phased context specialization is only worth it once paired with the strongest export lane.
slots.validates: Context-stage gains survive rebasing onto a serious deploy path.
slots.falsifies: The context-stage story is too weak to survive export alignment.

slots.name: alternating_objective_control_base
slots.why: Control for objective-microcycle hypotheses.
slots.validates: Alternating objective work is measured against the base trunk.
slots.falsifies: This lane lacks a stable anchor.

slots.name: alternating_objective_control_repeat
slots.why: Noise floor for objective-microcycle work.
slots.validates: Small alternating-objective deltas are interpretable.
slots.falsifies: This lane is too noisy to rank.

slots.name: alternating_objective_late_qat_microcycle
slots.why: Runs normal steps most of the time but interleaves sparse deploy-aligned steps late.
slots.validates: Alternating objectives beats one blended objective.
slots.falsifies: Sparse objective cycling is too weak or too disruptive.

slots.name: alternating_objective_weight_noise_microcycle
slots.why: Alternates clean learning steps with sparse flatness-shaping steps instead of applying noise continuously.
slots.validates: The geometry story should be temporal and sparse, not homogeneous.
slots.falsifies: Continuous geometry pressure is still the only useful form.

slots.name: alternating_objective_export_surrogate
slots.why: Interleaves normal NLL with a sparse export-surrogate penalty instead of poisoning every step.
slots.validates: Export alignment should be pulsed rather than blended.
slots.falsifies: Export alignment needs to be continuously present to matter.

slots.name: alternating_objective_export_surrogate_soft
slots.why: Uses a softer export-surrogate pulse late to test whether the objective needs to be gentler than A4.
slots.validates: The right alternating export pressure is sparse and low-amplitude.
slots.falsifies: Only a stronger export surrogate is worth keeping.

slots.name: alternating_objective_late_qat_plus_ema
slots.why: Pairs sparse late deploy-aligned steps with exported-state selection.
slots.validates: Alternating objectives and state selection are complementary.
slots.falsifies: The objective-cycling gain vanishes once export state is smoothed.

slots.name: alternating_objective_dual_pulse
slots.why: Alternates clean steps, sparse deploy-aligned steps, and sparse flatness-shaping steps in separate pulses.
slots.validates: The late run should be decomposed into multiple specialized step types.
slots.falsifies: A multi-regime microcycle is too complex for the 600s horizon.

slots.name: moonshot_control_base
slots.why: Frontier-aligned 11L control for the moonshot pack.
slots.validates: The pack has a stable baseline on the merged root train_gpt.py.
slots.falsifies: Root instability or scheduler issues invalidate moonshot comparisons.

slots.name: moonshot_control_repeat
slots.why: Noise floor for the core moonshot pack.
slots.validates: Small deltas are interpretable.
slots.falsifies: This pack is too noisy for fine ranking.

slots.name: exact_overlap_eval
slots.why: Directly attacks score_bpb by using exact overlap-aware sliding evaluation.
slots.validates: L_eval is still first-order for us.
slots.falsifies: The remaining gap is mostly not evaluation policy.

slots.name: ema_export
slots.why: Tests whether deployed artifact selection beats raw checkpoint selection.
slots.validates: Late-phase averaging reduces deployed loss.
slots.falsifies: EMA does not move the deployed metric enough to matter.

slots.name: late_qat_active
slots.why: Pushes training late into export-aware weight states instead of only raw fp32 quality.
slots.validates: Active late QAT closes a real deployment gap.
slots.falsifies: The current export mismatch is too small or this route is too disruptive.

slots.name: countinit_bigram
slots.why: Injects explicit short-range statistics the trunk may underfit in 600 seconds.
slots.validates: A low-order prior is still missing from the model class.
slots.falsifies: The trunk already absorbs this structure quickly enough.

slots.name: countinit_bigram_plus_ema
slots.why: Tests whether explicit priors and deployed-checkpoint averaging stack cleanly.
slots.validates: The prior branch benefits from phase alignment rather than conflicting with it.
slots.falsifies: Any bigram win is too fragile to survive averaging.

slots.name: exact_overlap_eval_plus_ema
slots.why: Separates eval-policy gains from deployed-checkpoint gains and measures whether they add.
slots.validates: The score lift survives when both lanes are active.
slots.falsifies: One of the two is mostly shadowing the other.

slots.name: export_control_base
slots.why: Control for the deferred export lane.
slots.validates: The export-specific pack has a stable reference.
slots.falsifies: Export-only comparisons are too unstable to interpret.

slots.name: full_gptq_int6
slots.why: Highest-upside export patch from the moonshot curation.
slots.validates: Deployed quantization quality is the main remaining gap.
slots.falsifies: The deployment lane is not first-order for our setup.

slots.name: full_gptq_int6_plus_ema
slots.why: Tests whether EMA and full GPTQ stack after the main GPTQ port exists.
slots.validates: Phase alignment still matters after a strong export patch.
slots.falsifies: GPTQ dominates enough that EMA is redundant here.

slots.name: moonshot_second_wave_control
slots.why: Control for the second-wave moonshot pack.
slots.validates: The second-wave pack has a stable frontier-aligned base.
slots.falsifies: Second-wave comparisons are too noisy to trust.

slots.name: moonshot_second_wave_repeat
slots.why: Noise floor for the second-wave moonshot pack.
slots.validates: Small second-wave deltas are interpretable.
slots.falsifies: This pack needs more repeats before ranking.

slots.name: moonshot_xsa_all
slots.why: Promotes the architecture destination from XSA4 to XSA-all in the moonshot lane.
slots.validates: Broader context handling is a real high-upside lane.
slots.falsifies: The XSA-all story is overfit to upstream stacks.

slots.name: moonshot_curriculum_reverse
slots.why: Fast probe for whether shard order is first-order enough to matter in the moonshot lane.
slots.validates: The 600-second cap makes information order a real bottleneck.
slots.falsifies: Curriculum requires a smarter ranked schedule than simple reversal.

slots.name: moonshot_curriculum_size_desc
slots.why: Cheap heuristic order probe without requiring an external ranking file.
slots.validates: A rough curriculum heuristic is already enough to move the endpoint.
slots.falsifies: The curriculum lane depends on smarter external ranking.

slots.name: moonshot_xsa_all_plus_curriculum
slots.why: Tests whether the two strongest newly promoted non-export lanes stack.
slots.validates: Context and training-order improvements are distinct enough to compose.
slots.falsifies: One of the lanes is mostly proxying the other.

slots.name: moonshot_full_gptq_plus_xsa_all
slots.why: Tests whether stronger export and broader context reinforce each other in one stack.
slots.validates: The architecture lane needs the deployment lane to fully pay off.
slots.falsifies: One lane dominates and the stack is not worth the extra complexity.

slots.name: moonshot_full_gptq_xsa_all_ema
slots.why: Tests the main no-TTT moonshot composition now that all three pieces are runnable.
slots.validates: Export alignment, broader context, and EMA are jointly useful.
slots.falsifies: The stack is too interaction-sensitive to beat simpler children.

slots.name: moonshot_geometry_control
slots.why: Control for flat-minima and gradient-geometry moonshot screens.
slots.validates: The geometry moonshot pack has a stable frontier-aligned baseline.
slots.falsifies: This pack is too noisy to trust subtle deploy-quality effects.

slots.name: moonshot_geometry_repeat
slots.why: Noise floor for the geometry moonshot pack.
slots.validates: Small geometry deltas are interpretable.
slots.falsifies: This pack needs more repeats before ranking.

slots.name: moonshot_weight_perturbation
slots.why: Biases training toward flatter minima that may quantize unusually well, even if fp32 validation is neutral.
slots.validates: The strongest new stage3 signal transfers into the moonshot lane.
slots.falsifies: The stage3 result was local and does not generalize to the moonshot base.

slots.name: moonshot_grad_centralization
slots.why: Tests whether low-cost gradient preprocessing is a real remaining gain channel in the moonshot lane.
slots.validates: Gradient geometry can improve both learning and deployed quality without architectural changes.
slots.falsifies: The stage3 signal was too small to survive rebasing.

slots.name: moonshot_late_weight_perturbation
slots.why: Tests the hypothesis that noise is most useful late, as a compressibility-shaping mechanism rather than a full-run optimizer.
slots.validates: Late-only flatness bias beats or matches full-run noise.
slots.falsifies: The geometry effect needs to act throughout training.

slots.name: moonshot_weight_perturbation_plus_ema
slots.why: Tests whether flatter minima and EMA-based export selection reinforce each other.
slots.validates: The flatness win persists and strengthens after export smoothing.
slots.falsifies: EMA washes out the geometry win or is redundant with it.

slots.name: moonshot_grad_centralization_plus_ema
slots.why: Tests whether the geometry-side gain from gradient centralization survives export smoothing.
slots.validates: Gradient geometry and EMA attack distinct parts of the pipeline.
slots.falsifies: The centralization gain is too weak to survive averaging.

slots.name: moonshot_weight_perturbation_plus_grad_centralization
slots.why: Tests whether the two geometry signals compose into a stronger deploy-quality improvement.
slots.validates: Flat-minima search and gradient preprocessing are distinct enough to stack.
slots.falsifies: One effect subsumes the other or the interaction is destabilizing.

slots.name: reference_base_control
slots.why: Reference control so the hailmary stage has a local anchor against strong assembled stacks.
slots.validates: Distance-to-frontier claims are measured against the same local trunk.
slots.falsifies: Reference anchor noise is too high to use for progress tracking.

slots.name: moonshot_throughput_base_control
slots.why: Baseline for isolating whether FA3 buys useful extra steps in the moonshot lane.
slots.validates: The FA3 lane can be measured against the unchanged moonshot trunk.
slots.falsifies: FA3 effects are too noisy to interpret here.

slots.name: moonshot_throughput_base_plus_fa3
slots.why: Tests whether FA3 matters on the plain moonshot trunk.
slots.validates: There is a kernel-only throughput win worth caring about.
slots.falsifies: FA3 is neutral even before rebasing.

slots.name: moonshot_throughput_xsa_all_control
slots.why: Broader-context stack without FA3, so the FA3 delta is measured on a heavier attention path.
slots.validates: The XSA-all lane is heavy enough for kernel speedups to matter.
slots.falsifies: This stack is still not attention-bound enough for FA3 to pay off.

slots.name: moonshot_throughput_xsa_all_plus_fa3
slots.why: Tests whether FA3 helps most once the attention path is heavier.
slots.validates: Kernel speedups grow in value on the XSA-all lane.
slots.falsifies: FA3 does not materially help even a broader attention stack.

slots.name: moonshot_throughput_full_gptq_control
slots.why: Strong no-TTT export-aligned stack without FA3, to measure whether throughput survives rebasing.
slots.validates: The full-GPTQ lane is a meaningful place to test FA3.
slots.falsifies: The FA3 effect vanishes once the stack is strong.

slots.name: moonshot_throughput_full_gptq_plus_fa3
slots.why: Tests whether FA3 still helps when the main moonshot export lane is already in place.
slots.validates: Throughput gains survive rebasing onto the strongest export lane.
slots.falsifies: The FA3 gain is too small once the moonshot stack is strong.

slots.name: moonshot_throughput_full_gptq_xsa_all_control
slots.why: Heaviest no-TTT local stack without FA3, to test whether FA3 matters exactly where it should matter most.
slots.validates: A full no-TTT proxy is still sensitive to attention kernel throughput.
slots.falsifies: The full stack is bottlenecked elsewhere.

slots.name: moonshot_throughput_full_gptq_xsa_all_plus_fa3
slots.why: Tests FA3 on the heaviest local no-TTT moonshot stack.
slots.validates: FA3 is still useful at the exact stack depth where extra attention throughput should matter most.
slots.falsifies: FA3 is not the right systems multiplier for the current moonshot frontier proxy.

slots.name: reference_base_repeat
slots.why: Noise floor for the hailmary reference pack.
slots.validates: Small distance-to-anchor deltas are interpretable.
slots.falsifies: This anchor pack needs more repeats before ranking.

slots.name: merged_sota_proxy_a
slots.why: Best locally reproducible merged-SOTA-like stack inside hailmary.
slots.validates: Moonshot results can be compared against a strong merged-SOTA proxy, not only against the base trunk.
slots.falsifies: The merged-SOTA proxy is too weak or unstable to use as an anchor.
notes: Proxy, not an exact reconstruction of the merged leaderboard stack.

slots.name: merged_sota_proxy_b
slots.why: Repeat of the merged-SOTA-like proxy for local noise estimation.
slots.validates: Distance-to-merged-proxy claims exceed rerun noise.
slots.falsifies: The merged proxy itself is too noisy to be a useful bar.
notes: Proxy, not an exact reconstruction of the merged leaderboard stack.

slots.name: frontier_no_ttt_proxy_a
slots.why: Best locally reproducible no-TTT frontier-like stack in hailmary, including full GPTQ.
slots.validates: Moonshot winners can be measured against a serious assembled frontier proxy.
slots.falsifies: The full-GPTQ frontier proxy is too unstable to serve as a bar.
notes: Proxy only. The exact public frontier may still differ in data order and export details.

slots.name: frontier_no_ttt_proxy_b
slots.why: Repeat of the hailmary no-TTT frontier proxy.
slots.validates: Distance-to-frontier-proxy claims exceed rerun noise.
slots.falsifies: The frontier proxy is too noisy to use for progress tracking.
notes: Proxy only. The exact public frontier may still differ in data order and export details.

slots.name: frontier_no_ttt_proxy_plus_weight_perturbation
slots.why: Tests whether the geometry-based flatness win still helps on top of the strongest local no-TTT proxy.
slots.validates: Weight perturbation improves a strong assembled frontier-like stack, not just weaker trunks.
slots.falsifies: The geometry win disappears once the stack is already strong.

slots.name: frontier_no_ttt_proxy_plus_grad_centralization
slots.why: Tests whether gradient geometry remains useful once the strongest local no-TTT stack is already present.
slots.validates: Gradient preprocessing still moves a near-frontier proxy.
slots.falsifies: The centralization gain was only a weak-trunk effect.
